pubmed_links: [Kang et al. (2025) Aberrant ERK signaling in astrocytes impairs learning and memory in RASopathy-associated BRAF mutant mouse models](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39964758/); [Pierpont et al. (2022) Neurologic and neurodevelopmental complications in cardiofaciocutaneous syndrome are associated with genotype](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/35524774/); [Kim & Baek (2019) Neurodevelopmental aspects of RASopathies](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/31250618/)
summary: BRAF germline mutations cause cardiofaciocutaneous syndrome, which features intellectual disability (~82%) and seizures (~55%), and de novo BRAF variants have been identified in autism spectrum disorder sequencing cohorts; mechanistic mouse studies show that aberrant ERK signaling in BRAF-mutant astrocytes impairs learning and memory, providing a cellular basis for cognitive deficits. (emerging evidence)
